selfcheck.question: Si una función no tiene return, ¿qué devuelve la llamada?
selfcheck.justification_from_packet: Skeptic: 'Definición, llamada y retorno' — without explicit return, Python returns None. Not 0/False/always-error.

selfcheck.question: ¿Por qué `def f(xs=[])` es peligroso?
selfcheck.chosen_text: El default mutable se comparte entre llamadas
selfcheck.justification_from_packet: Skeptic: defaults are evaluated once at definition; never use mutable list default def f(xs=[]) — shared across calls. Use None and create inside.

selfcheck.question: Una función pura…
selfcheck.chosen_text: Mismo input → mismo output, sin efectos colaterales
selfcheck.justification_from_packet: Skeptic: pure function = same args → same result, no side effects (no print/disk/globals). Printing or reading global config breaks purity in this packet.

selfcheck.question: LEGB significa…
selfcheck.justification_from_packet: Skeptic: LEGB section expands Local, Enclosing, Global, Builtin as name lookup order. Other expansions are invented nonsense.

selfcheck.question: Idempotencia de un normalizador f significa…
selfcheck.chosen_text: f(f(x)) == f(x) para entradas del dominio
selfcheck.justification_from_packet: Skeptic: normalizers must be pure and idempotent: f(f(x)) == f(x) for valid inputs. Not 'runs once in process lifetime'.

retrospection.note: Knowledge limited to sequential packets through active section.
